# OverlapTransformer: An Efficient and Yaw-Angle-Invariant Transformer Network for LiDAR-Based Place Recognition

- **Work ID:** `LPR-046`

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** KITTI Odometry, Ford Campus, Haomo — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5–7, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-7;Table I-V
- **input_unit:** single LiDAR scan projected to a 64x900 or 32x900 range image — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–4, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-7;Table I-V
- **invariances:** yaw rotation, architecturally derived up to range-image discretization, point-order permutation in NetVLAD aggregation — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 3–5, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-7;Table I-V
- **representation:** single-channel spherical range image, horizontal-shift-equivariant convolutional encoder, self-attention transformer, NetVLAD and MLP global descriptor, 256-D Euclidean retrieval, overlap-supervised lazy triplet loss — `source_fact`; manual, p. 2–5, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-7;Table I-V
- **uses_learning:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 1–8, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-7;Table I-V
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–8, § Abstract;III. Method;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-7;Table I-V
